# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:2.7-slim

ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
    PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 \
    PIP_NO_CACHE_DIR=1 \
    PIP_DISABLE_PIP_VERSION_CHECK=1

RUN groupadd -r appuser --gid=1000 && \
    useradd -r -g appuser --uid=1000 --no-log-init --create-home appuser

RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    gcc \
    libc6-dev \
    libssl-dev \
    libffi-dev \
    ca-certificates && \
    apt-get clean && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* /tmp/* /var/tmp/*

WORKDIR /app

COPY requirements.txt .

RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade pip==20.3.4 setuptools==44.1.1 wheel==0.37.1 && \
    pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

COPY --chown=appuser:appuser . .

RUN find /app -type d -exec chmod 755 {} \; && \
    find /app -type f -exec chmod 644 {} \; && \
    chmod -R 755 /home/appuser

RUN rm -rf /root/.cache /tmp/* /var/tmp/*

USER appuser

EXPOSE 8000

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD python -c "import urllib2; urllib2.urlopen('http://localhost:8000/health').read()" || exit 1

CMD ["python", "app.py"]
